context: This chapter describes the sacred rites to be performed when a person is approaching death. It covers placing the dying on sacred ground, chanting of divine names, the importance of dying with awareness of God, charity at the time of death, and the proper conduct of family members during the final moments.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
मरणासन्नदेहस्य कर्तव्यं किं विधीयते ।
कथं मृत्युमुपागम्य सद्गतिं लभते नरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
maraṇāsanna-dehasya kartavyaṃ kiṃ vidhīyate |
kathaṃ mṛtyum upāgamya sadgatiṃ labhate naraḥ ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: What rites are prescribed for one whose body is approaching death? How does a man, having met death, obtain a good destination?

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
श्रृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि यत् कार्यं मरणे सति ।
मरणासन्नदेहस्य कृत्यमेतत् सनातनम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi yat kāryaṃ maraṇe sati |
maraṇāsanna-dehasya kṛtyam etat sanātanam ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya, I shall tell you what must be done when death is at hand. These are the eternal rites for one whose body is nearing death.

verses.hindi:
मृत्युकाले तु सम्प्राप्ते शुचौ देशे समाहितः ।
गोमयेनोपलिप्ताय भूमौ संस्थाप्य तं नरम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
mṛtyu-kāle tu samprāpte śucau deśe samāhitaḥ |
gomayenopaliptāya bhūmau saṃsthāpya taṃ naram ||
verses.translation: When the time of death has arrived, with concentration, the person should be placed upon the ground in a clean place smeared with cow-dung.

verses.hindi:
तुलसीकाष्ठजं शय्यां कुशसंस्तरणान्विताम् ।
भूमौ विन्यस्य तत्रैव शाययेत् तं मुमूर्षुकम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
tulasī-kāṣṭha-jaṃ śayyāṃ kuśa-saṃstaraṇānvitām |
bhūmau vinyasya tatraiva śāyayet taṃ mumūrṣukam ||
verses.translation: A bed made of tulasi wood, spread with kusha grass, should be placed on the ground, and the dying person should be laid upon it.

verses.hindi:
पल्यङ्के मरणं यस्य गृहे वा शयनोपरि ।
प्रेतो भवति सो ज्ञेयो भूमौ मृत्युः शुभावहः ॥
verses.transliteration:
palyaṅke maraṇaṃ yasya gṛhe vā śayanopari |
preto bhavati so jñeyo bhūmau mṛtyuḥ śubhāvahaḥ ||
verses.translation: He who dies on a cot or bed in a house becomes a ghost — know this. Death upon the ground is auspicious.

verses.hindi:
गङ्गाजलं तुलसीपत्रमिश्रितम् तस्य वक्त्रे प्रदातव्यम् ।
चरणामृतमीशस्य सर्वपापविनाशनम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Gaṅgā-jalaṃ tulasī-patra-miśritam tasya vaktre pradātavyam |
caraṇāmṛtam Īśasya sarva-pāpa-vināśanam ||
verses.translation: Water of the Ganges mixed with tulasi leaves should be poured into his mouth — the nectar from the feet of the Lord, which destroys all sins.

verses.hindi:
तस्य कर्णद्वये विप्रो रामनाम जपेत् सदा ।
श्रीकृष्ण श्रीकृष्ण इति वदेद् वा मुक्तिकाम्यया ॥
verses.transliteration:
tasya karṇa-dvaye vipro Rāma-nāma japet sadā |
Śrī-Kṛṣṇa Śrī-Kṛṣṇa iti vaded vā mukti-kāmyayā ||
verses.translation: A Brahmana should constantly chant the name of Rama in both ears of the dying person, or say 'Sri Krishna, Sri Krishna' for the sake of his liberation.

verses.hindi:
अन्तकालेऽपि यो विष्णोर्नामग्रहणमाचरेत् ।
स मुक्तिं लभते मर्त्यो मम लोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
anta-kāle'pi yo Viṣṇor nāma-grahaṇam ācaret |
sa muktiṃ labhate martyo mama lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: Even at the final hour, he who takes the name of Vishnu — that mortal obtains liberation and goes to My world.

verses.hindi:
यं यं वाऽपि स्मरन् भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् ।
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
yaṃ yaṃ vā'pi smaran bhāvaṃ tyajaty ante kalevaram |
taṃ tam evaiti Kaunteya sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ ||
verses.translation: Whatever state of being one remembers when leaving the body at the end, to that very state one goes, O son of Kunti, being always absorbed in that thought.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च ।
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन् यः प्रजहाति सः ॥
verses.transliteration:
tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu mām anusmara yudhya ca |
anta-kāle ca mām eva smaran yaḥ prajahāti saḥ ||
verses.translation: Therefore, at all times remember Me alone. He who, remembering Me at the final moment, departs — he comes to Me.

verses.hindi:
शालग्रामशिलां तस्य शरीरोपरि विन्यसेत् ।
ललाटे तिलकं चैव नारायणमुदाहरेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
śālagrāma-śilāṃ tasya śarīropari vinyaset |
lalāṭe tilakaṃ caiva Nārāyaṇam udāharet ||
verses.translation: The Shalagrama stone should be placed on his body, a tilaka mark on his forehead, and the name of Narayana should be recited.

verses.hindi:
ऊर्ध्वपुण्ड्रं ललाटे च तुलसीमालिकां गले ।
धारयेत् मरणासन्नो विष्णुभक्तो भवेन्नरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
ūrdhva-puṇḍraṃ lalāṭe ca tulasī-mālikāṃ gale |
dhārayet maraṇāsanno Viṣṇu-bhakto bhaven naraḥ ||
verses.translation: The dying person should wear the upward mark on his forehead and a tulasi rosary around his neck, thus becoming a devotee of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
श्रीमद्भागवतं गीतां पुराणं गारुडं तथा ।
श्रावयेत् मरणासन्नं श्रवणात् पापनाशनम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrīmad-Bhāgavataṃ Gītāṃ purāṇaṃ Gāruḍaṃ tathā |
śrāvayet maraṇāsannaṃ śravaṇāt pāpa-nāśanam ||
verses.translation: The Srimad Bhagavatam, the Gita, and the Garuda Purana should be recited to the dying person; by hearing them, sins are destroyed.

verses.hindi:
दानं देयं यथाशक्ति ब्राह्मणेभ्यो विशेषतः ।
सुवर्णं रजतं वस्त्रं गां तिलान् दक्षिणां तथा ॥
verses.transliteration:
dānaṃ deyaṃ yathā-śakti brāhmaṇebhyo viśeṣataḥ |
suvarṇaṃ rajataṃ vastraṃ gāṃ tilān dakṣiṇāṃ tathā ||
verses.translation: Gifts should be given according to one's capacity, especially to Brahmanas — gold, silver, clothing, a cow, sesame, and fees.

verses.hindi:
दीपं ज्वालयेत् सम्यक् घृतपूर्णं विशेषतः ।
तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय इति मन्त्रेण दीपयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
dīpaṃ jvālayet samyak ghṛta-pūrṇaṃ viśeṣataḥ |
tamaso mā jyotir gamaya iti mantreṇa dīpayet ||
verses.translation: A lamp filled with ghee should be properly lit, especially with the mantra 'Lead me from darkness to light.'

verses.hindi:
शङ्खध्वनिं प्रकुर्वीत हरिनामकीर्तनम् ।
कुर्वन्तु बान्धवाः सर्वे यावत् प्राणो विनिर्गमेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
śaṅkha-dhvaniṃ prakurvīta hari-nāma-kīrtanam |
kurvantu bāndhavāḥ sarve yāvat prāṇo vinirgamet ||
verses.translation: The conch should be sounded and the chanting of Hari's name should be performed. All relatives should continue this until the life-breath departs.

verses.hindi:
न रोदनं प्रकुर्वीत न च क्रन्दनमाचरेत् ।
शान्तचित्तेन सम्बोध्यो मरणासन्नको नरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
na rodanaṃ prakurvīta na ca krandanam ācaret |
śānta-cittena sambodhyo maraṇāsannako naraḥ ||
verses.translation: One should not weep or wail. The dying person should be awakened to consciousness with a calm mind.

verses.hindi:
रुदन्ति बान्धवा यत्र मृत्युकाले नरस्य तु ।
तत्र प्रेतो भयं प्राप्य सद्गतिं नैव गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
rudanti bāndhavā yatra mṛtyu-kāle narasya tu |
tatra preto bhayaṃ prāpya sad-gatiṃ naiva gacchati ||
verses.translation: Where relatives weep at the time of a person's death, the departed soul, seized by fear, does not attain a good destination.

verses.hindi:
प्राणे निर्गच्छति यदा तस्य नामानि कीर्तयेत् ।
गोविन्द गोविन्द इति वदेत् प्राणविमोचने ॥
verses.transliteration:
prāṇe nirgacchati yadā tasya nāmāni kīrtayet |
Govinda Govinda iti vadet prāṇa-vimocane ||
verses.translation: When the life-breath is departing, one should chant His names. 'Govinda, Govinda' — this should be said at the release of the life-breath.

verses.hindi:
एवं कृते विधानेन मृतः स्वर्गतिमाप्नुयात् ।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो वैकुण्ठे मोदते चिरम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
evaṃ kṛte vidhānena mṛtaḥ svarga-gatim āpnuyāt |
sarva-pāpa-vinirmukto Vaikuṇṭhe modate ciram ||
verses.translation: When these rites are thus performed, the deceased attains the heavenly path. Freed from all sins, he rejoices in Vaikuntha for a long time.

verses.hindi:
यो नरो विष्णुभक्त्या तु मृत्युकाले हरिं स्मरेत् ।
न तस्य यमदूतानां भयं भवति कर्हिचित् ॥
verses.transliteration:
yo naro Viṣṇu-bhaktyā tu mṛtyu-kāle Hariṃ smaret |
na tasya yama-dūtānāṃ bhayaṃ bhavati karhicit ||
verses.translation: The man who, through devotion to Vishnu, remembers Hari at the time of death — for him there is never any fear of the messengers of Yama.

verses.hindi:
विष्णुदूताः समायान्ति दिव्यरूपा मनोहराः ।
स्वर्णविमानमारोप्य नयन्ति परमं पदम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Viṣṇu-dūtāḥ samāyānti divya-rūpā manoharāḥ |
svarṇa-vimānam āropya nayanti paramaṃ padam ||
verses.translation: The messengers of Vishnu come, divine in form and beautiful. Placing him upon a golden aerial chariot, they lead him to the supreme abode.
